transcript: Hi! I’m not seeing anything that we have or have had that just had the title “Aardvark”. Could it be a different title maybe?
I’m lookin’ up “Loan Shark” in my inventory and nothing’s popping up on that.
I’m sorry, ah, were you looking for a book called “Aardvark”?
No, I s—I—I’m sorry. I got “Loan Shark” from that. ’S my—that’s my bad! “Aardvark”. Okay. That is on me! Pardon me.
I have “Aardvark is Ready for War”—is what’s poppin’ up.
Yeah, again, ah, somebody called me. This is Defunct Books in Nashville.
Y—this is Lemuria Book Store in Jackon. I just had someone on the phone; he was looking for a book called “Aardvark”.
This is Square Books right here—
—Yeah. I’m confused as to why you’re calling me.
[“Heather” of Fox Den Books enters call]
This is Heather. How may I help you? Hello?
Are we—hello? Are we getting… Wow, what the heck’s goin’ on here?
Ah, this is Heather from Fox Den Books. Did you call us?—
—[chuckles] No! This is Brian from Chapter 2 Books in Hudson.
Somebody just called me looking for a book.
Okay! And where do I come into it? [laughs]—
—[speaking to a co-worker] did you just call Chapter 2 Books? [to conference] Nope! Wasn’t us!—
[“Chris” of Bowell Book Company enters call]
Yep. Yep! I hope you’re havin’ a great day!
[chuckles] You too! This is weird! I still got somebody on the other line! What—what is going on here?—
—Yeah! I don’t know but James was saying—
—this is happening to one of our employees this morning! That—
—there’s this customer calling and has multiple people on the line—
—I don’t know how they’re doing it but it’s—
—led to a lot of confusion. So I’m just gonna take us out of the equation!
Hey, I’m Chris at Boswell Book Company. Are you at a book store?
Yeah! Chapter 2 Books in Hudson, Wisconsin.
Okay! We’ve talked to people at, ah, The Attic in Green Bay, too—where we’ve had this—and I don’t know who else was on the line just now, but yeah. [inhales]
—So you called here looking for a book?—
I did not place the call. You guys, someone else is placing the call right now—
—Hi! This is Chris at Boswell Book Company—
—Yeah, have you guys gotten, ah, calls about “Baboon”?
You know, if we wait long enough, we might be able to get the whole population of Wisconsin on this call!
—I don’t know what go. So is—who’s meaning to call Downtown Books?
—Yeah, no. No one. I’ve been get—been getting these too. This is Chris at Boswell.
Yeah, I don’t know. Ah, I—I don’t know what’s going, either. Yeah. Uh, this is Downtown Book Store. Yeah—

—Um, yeah, this is—this is Lemuria Book Store. Um, yeah, we had a call earlier about somebody wanting “Aardvark”, um, and I think we hung up. But after we realized, like, everyone was on it. But, um, yeah, I don’t know what’s goin’ on. This is very weird!
—Don’t these—don’t these people have anything better to do?—
—I don’t know! It seems like it’s going to keep going until—uh, I have no idea.
Okay, alright. Well if no one’s intending to call Downtown Books then I’ll just hang up [chuckles]
Yeah! I’m gonna do the same. Good luck, guys!—
—I guess we’re trying to find a—a book for you called “Baboon”?
Um, you—you’ve called The Book Store.
What book store are you talkin’ about?
Kinokuniya, the book store in Japantown.
Uh, you’re supposed to help your friend.
I do—I don’t understand what your friend would like to get—what kind of book he wants.
—He wants a—yeah, he wants a book: “A-A-R-D-V-A-R-K”, correct?
I—I apologize. Um. Yeah, this is Boswell Book Company. Someone has been calling around.
Are you calling about a book store?
What book store are you talkin’ about?
—Did someone call you asking about a book?
What kind of book you talkin’ about?
—He’s asking, uh, that—his name is Joe. He asking for book and he spell me the name: “A-A-R-D-V-A-R-K”. And that you’re supposed to give me ISBN number so that I can find whether we have it.
You know something? Y—y—you know something—what I wanna tell you? You’re a criminal and you’re stupid! You’re a very big criminal! You sonofabitch!
My goodness! I don’t know. That’s a customer who called me asking for this book. I don’t know what he’s talking about—
—Okay! Sorry about that! I apologize anything wrong from my side.
You’re supposed to be helping me figure out which “Aardvark” book I’m looking for?
Uh, [chuckles] n—well, I can. I own a bookstore; Smith & Hannon.
Um, I got a call from somebody who said that they were getting somebody else on the phone.
I was supposed to be looking for a book called “Aardvark” but the last person I talked to didn’t have enough information about it for me to find it.
What kinda book you talkin’ about? Just a minute!—
—Okay, I don’t know what’s goin’ on but I was told—
—You’re full of crap! You’re criminals! You’re criminals! You’re criminals!—
—Thank you for calling First Edition Rare Books! Please hold while we connect your call!
—[coughs]—if any of you are ever in Milwaukee’s General Mitchell Field, we have the world’s first used book store in an airport and you’re all welcome—
—Did you mean to call Annie Bloom’s Books in Portland, Oregon?
—Who are—are you talkin’ to me? Who are you—who are you?
I’m Mike at the b—at The Rennaisance in Milwaukee and, uh, the other lady is Katie at Annie Bloom Books in Portland. And how we all ended up on lines together I don’t know! [chuckles]—

—Well let me tell you—let me tell you who you are! You’re a motherfuckin chickenshit criminal! You’ll be in jail! You piece of Shit! Shit! Shit! You’re a cancer for America! You’re a piece of Shit! Shit! Shit!
—apparently, uh, someone from the, uh, Trump campaign is on the line!

—hear what I said? We have 150,000 books in this store! I—I don’t know what kind of book it is, really. When you say, “Nonfiction” [inhales] we have nonfiction African-American, Asian-American, Hispanic-American, gay, lesbian, transgender, labor, radical Left movements, Rightwing, Asian languages. Do you understand the problem with looking to see if it’s there? We have 150,000 books on the whole top floor. What is it about?
Hi! What is this book you’re looking for?
What is the book that you are looking for, here?
Did I ever tell you about a book? What are you—
You said you picked up a book called “Aardvark” at our shop!
Where are you?! What shop are you in?
You’re a motherfuckin’ criminal! You piece of shit! You’re gonna be in jail very soon! You sonofabitch!
Okay. Um. So, uh, I understand you’re trying to find a book called “Baboon”.
Yeah, I don’t know what you’re talking about.
—Uh [chuckles] I was speaking to someone on the phone who said they were gonna go get someone who knew more about this book they were asking about. Are you that person?—
—Ye—No, I don’t know. It’s weird. Who’re you calling, Boswell Books or Downtown Books?
Okay, um [chuckles] I am in a book store. Someone—p—presumably a customer—was calling and asking if we have our copies of this book called “Baboon” and it’s not in our computer, so—

—Yeah, I don’t—It must be—it must be Boswell Books, I don’t know. Because this is Downtown Books and for some reason we’re getting calls that are intended for Boswell over here. It’s very bizarre! I don’t know if they did something where they—
[“Woman(5) of Mystery to Me enters call]
—forwarded a call or something. I don’t know.
Yeah, is there somebody else on the line now?
—I just heard a third voice—a woman—but—
—but the thing is, uh, it must be someone else—
—It must be for a different store—
Yeah—there—hold on. There’s someone else on the line now. Listen to them. Okay. Go ‘head. Is this “Bryan”?
Uh, this is Ryan at Book Soup. H—how can I help you?—
—Okay, there’s—I’m gonna hang up because this has clearly nothing to do with our store.
Okay. Alright. So I don’t know what’s goin’ on—w—there seems to be stuff goin’ on. This is Book Soup?
[smiling] Okay! That’s even more strange! Is Book Soup on the line?—
—W—w—w—why am I a part of this phone call?—
[“Man(14)” of Stories Books & Cafe enters call]
There seems to be a whole bunch of different people on this line from—from different bookstores. Very bizarre!
Alright, uh, is there somethin’ I can help you with, or…?
—Alright, I’m gonna hang up. You can call us back—
Okay. Alright. I’m—I guess I’m gonna hang up too, ‘cause it doesn’t—I’m not looking for a book, I’m a book store!
[speaking to “Man(15)”, who is present] There’s somethin’ bizarre goin’ on. Who is this? There’s somethin’ bizarre gion’ on—
[“Matt” of Mystery to Me enters call]
—with the phone system here. What’s that?
Mystery to Me! This is Matt!
—Yeah, so, do you think I called you?
—Well, yeah. I just got a call from—
—No! Now there’s three of us on the line, right? Somethin’ very strange is goin’ on, um—
—where there—there seems to be all these—where are you from?
[“Woman(7)” of Boswell Book Company enters call]
—I’ve—I’ve heard this is a scam. So [sneers]
Everyone hang up! Stop doing this to people, okay? Hang up! Stop calling, okay?
Thanks for calling Posman Books where we offer no books on baboons! How can I help you?
[laughs] Stop calling''! Okay? We don’t have time for this.
[“Mina” of Kinokuniya Santa Monica enters call]
Thank you for calling Kinokuniya Santa Monica. This is Mina—
—No, hang up! Hang up! Hang up!—
